burgers.name: Whopper
burgers.desc: Burger King's signature hamburger, introduced in 1957, featuring a 4 oz (110 g) flame-grilled beef patty, mayonnaise, lettuce, tomato, pickles, ketchup, and sliced onions on a toasted sesame seed bun.
burgers.review: The first creation of the Saucefather and an all time classic. Legend says that he defeated Master Ronald with this in the First Great Burger War.

burgers.name: Double Whopper
burgers.desc: The Double Whopper is designed for those who want more meat from the original Whopper without changing the classic flavor profile. Features 2 flame-grilled beef patties and the same fresh toppings.
burgers.review: The Double Whopper is the elder brother of the Whopper. Said to have fought fiercely against Biggus Mac, the leader of the MacDonalds, in the Second Great Burger War.

burgers.name: Triple Whopper
burgers.desc: The Triple Whopper is designed for those who want even more meat from the original Whopper. 3 delicious flame-grilled beef patties and the usual fresh toppings.
burgers.review: The Triple Whopper- originally thought to be only a myth- is the strongest of the original Whopper bloodline. It single-handedly struck down the third of the Wendy's triplets, "Dave's Triple", in the Third Great Burger War.

burgers.name: Whopper Jr.
burgers.desc: The Whopper Jr. is a smaller version of the original Whopper, designed for those who want the classic flavor in a more compact size. It features a smaller flame-grilled beef patty, but the same fresh toppings.
burgers.review: The Whopper Jr. is the younger sibling of the Whopper, often underestimated but still packing a punch. It played a crucial role in the Third Great Burger War, where it outsmarted the MacDonald hamburger troops in a battle of wits and flavor.
